Class CombatController

java.lang.Object
java.util.Observable
controller.CombatController
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class CombatController extends Observable implements Serializable
The Controller following the MVC architecture that allows the view to cleanly interact with the various game elements. Methods are implemented to allow the view to make a move for one of the two players' active Mon, Retrieve either player's active Mon's movelist, switch the active Mon for a given player as well as allow a computer player to take an action.
Author:
Isa Abdulrazaq, Matt Lagier
See Also:

    static enum 
    CombatController.Difficulty
    Enum that controls which version of the computer AI is used by the CombatController class.
    class

    ReadWriteServer handles the clients networking logic.

  • Constructor Details

    • CombatController

      public CombatController(Player p1, Player p2, CombatController.Difficulty diff)
      Constructor that takes references to the two player objects that will be involved in a given fight. The Player class holds all the relevant data for each player's team
      Parameters:
      p1 - In a singleplayer game, represents the player. In multiplayer, represents the server host.
      p2 - In a singleplayer game, represents the computer player. In multiplayer, represents the server client.
      diff - enum which describes which AI variant will be used by makeComputerMove()

    • CombatController

      public CombatController(Player p1, Player p2)
      Deprecated.
      Please use CombatController(Player, Player, Difficulty). Constructor that takes references to the two player objects that will be involved in a given fight. The Player class holds all the relevant data for each player's team
      Parameters:
      p1 - In a singleplayer game, represents the player. In multiplayer, represents the server host.
      p2 - In a singleplayer game, represents the computer player. In multiplayer, represents the server client.

  • Method Details

    • getPlayerMoves

      public Move[] getPlayerMoves(int player)
      Convenience method to get all the moves associated the a player's active Mon. If an invalid player param is passed, an exception is thrown. In gameplay, this exception should never come up.
      Parameters:
      player - either 1 or 2 depending on whether the movelist for p1 or p2's active mon is desired.
      Returns:
      Move[] array containing the 4 (max) moves associated with a player's active mon.

    • makeMove

      public void makeMove(int player, int moveIndex)
      Causes a player's active Mon to use the move given as a param and apply the relevant effect associated with it (typically damaging the other Mon). Notifies observers (the view when a change is made to the Player objects and by extension their active Mons.
      Parameters:
      player - the player making the move (either 1 or 2)
      moveIndex - the index in the Player's active Mon's movelist array corresponding to the selected move. (0-3)
      Throws:
      InterruptedException

    • switchMon

      public void switchMon(int player, int index)
      Calls the player object's Player.switchMon(int) method on the appropriate player. Notifies observers (the view when a change is made to the Player objects and by extension their active Mons.
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose active Mon is to be switched
      index - the index of the Mon to switch to

    • makeComputerMove

      public void makeComputerMove()
      Attempts to make a valid move for p2. If no valid move is found (i.e p2 has lost), no move is made. If this method is called while p2's active Mon has fainted, it will switch to the next valid mon before making a move. Selects a move based on the diff enum. easy = selects the weakest power move. medium = selects a random move. hard = selects the strongest power move. Previous implementation only supported hard AI.

    • getBestMove

      private int getBestMove()
      Finds the move with the highest power for p2.
      Returns:
      moveIndex the index of the move with the highest power.

    • getWorstMove

      private int getWorstMove()
      Finds the move with the lowest power for p2.
      Returns:
      moveIndex the index of the move with the lowest power.

    • getRandomMove

      private int getRandomMove()
      Selects a random move for p2 to use.
      Returns:
      moveIndex the index of randomly selected move.

    • getP1

      public Player getP1()
      Returns a reference to the human player/server host
      Returns:
      p1

    • getP2

      public Player getP2()
      Returns a reference to the computer player/client player
      Returns:
      p2

    • setP1

      public void setP1(Player player)
      Changes the reference to the human player/server host
      Parameters:
      player - a Player object that will hold the new human player/server host's team

    • setP2

      public void setP2(Player player)
      Changes the reference to the computer player/client player
      Parameters:
      player - a Player object that will hold the new computer player/server client's team

    • getMultiplayer

      public boolean getMultiplayer()
      Returns a boolean that identifies if the match is a multiplayer game or not, primarily used to identify a local match in combination with the networkingMultiplayer boolean.
      Returns:
      True if multiplayer, false if singleplayer

    • setMultiplayer

      public void setMultiplayer(boolean mp)
      Sets the multiplayer boolean as required.
      Parameters:
      mp - the boolean value that isMultiplayer should be set to.

    • getPlayerTurn

      public boolean getPlayerTurn()
      Returns a boolean value if it's the players turn.
      Returns:
      True if players turn, false otherwise.

    • setPlayerTurn

      public void setPlayerTurn(boolean turn)
      Sets the current player turn boolean
      Parameters:
      turn - Sets player1Turn to the boolean provided.

    • swapPlayerTurn

      public void swapPlayerTurn()
      Swaps the player1Turn boolean, if its a networking game we update the thread with the boolean value

    • setNetworkMultiplayer

      public void setNetworkMultiplayer(boolean mp)
      Sets the networkingMultiplayer boolean to the provided boolean value.
      Parameters:
      mp -

    • updateView

      public void updateView()
      Update call the notify observers function when the thread needs to update the view and has passed that information to the controller.

    • setPlayerID

      public void setPlayerID(int id)
      Set the players ID, used to determine which player is which in networking games and to determine which branch of code the thread should run.
      Parameters:
      id - integer value of the order in which players connect to the server

    • getPlayerID

      public int getPlayerID()
      Returns the integer value of the player ID
      Returns:

    • switchGameOver

      public void switchGameOver()
      Switch the gameOver boolean when the match is ended. Used to stop the update() function in the view from displaying two game over screens since the thread calls the update an extra time function too quick.

    • getGameOver

      public boolean getGameOver()
      Returns the value of the gameOver boolean
      Returns:
      True means the game is over, false means its still in progress.

    • getNetworkMultiplayer

      public boolean getNetworkMultiplayer()
      Returns the boolean value networkingMultiplayer which is used to determine if the game is a networking based multiplayer match. Used in conjunction with isNetworking to determine if the game is local or networked as well as separate it from the singleplayer.
      Returns:
      True if networking, false otherwise.

    • updateRWR

      public void updateRWR(CombatController.ReadWriteServer rwr)
      Update the controller with the thread object running the networking for the client.
      Parameters:
      rwr - The object currently running the thread.

    • getRWR

      public CombatController.ReadWriteServer getRWR()
      Returns the object currently running the thread.
      Returns:
      ReadWriteServer object running the networking thread.

    • updatePlayers

      public void updatePlayers()
      Updates the controller players with the players stored in the networking thread.

    • updateThread

      public void updateThread()
      Updates the thread with the current players stored in the controller.

    • waitReadWrite

      public void waitReadWrite() throws InterruptedException
      Forces a thread to wait, used to ensure the server is "turn" based
      Throws:
      InterruptedException

    • notifyReadWrite

      public void notifyReadWrite() throws InterruptedException
      Releases the thread from its wait status to continue processing commands.
      Throws:
      InterruptedException

    • setReadWriteThread

      public void setReadWriteThread(Thread rwt)
      Adds the thread object to the controller.
      Parameters:
      rwt -

    • getMPTurn

      public boolean getMPTurn()
      Returns the turn that is used by the thread to determine whose turn it is. Used for networking matches.
      Returns:
      True if player turn, false otherwise.

    • setMPTurn

      public void setMPTurn(boolean turn)
      Sets the MPTurn boolean based on the provided argument
      Parameters:
      turn -

    • connectToServer

      public void connectToServer(String ipAddress)
      connectToServer facilitates the clients connection to the server. It takes an ipAddress as input and tries to connect. It then gets its assigned ID, then creates a thread that runs the networking code in the background.
      Parameters:
      ipAddress -
